content.problemStatement: Write a C program that takes an integer as input and prints its reverse. Use a while loop for the reversal process.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the integer obtained by reversing the decimal digits of N while preserving its sign, each on a new line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: 10001 is a palindrome, so its reverse is the same.

solution.approach: We process each test case independently. The sign of the number is stored and the absolute value is taken. Using a while loop we repeatedly extract the last digit (num % 10) and build the reversed number by rev = rev * 10 + digit. After the loop the sign is reapplied. This runs in O(number of digits) time per test case and uses O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * log10 |N|) – each digit is visited once.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    t = int(data[0])
    out = []
    idx = 1
    for _ in range(t):
        num = int(data[idx]); idx += 1
        sign = -1 if num < 0 else 1
        num = abs(num)
        rev = 0
        while num:
            rev = rev * 10 + num % 10
            num //= 10
        out.append(str(sign * rev))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
